meaning: vt.滋养,使健壮,怀有(希望,仇恨等)
examples: As Tony says, "Depression strips you of any desire to nourish yourself, in any form."
Warm porridge could help increase one's personal heat and nourish the body in cold weather.
By investing in education, we nourish the talents of our children.
This keeps the lining of the uterus healthy and ready to nourish a developing foetus.
Plankton found in the estuaries nourish organisms all the way up the food chain.
